title: sorghum vulgare subsp. bicolor
intro: Sorghum vulgare subsp. bicolor, also known as broom sorghum, is a type of drought-tolerant grass native to Africa and Asia, characterized by its distinctive panicle and yellow or purple flowers. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine due to its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of broom sorghum are thought to include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular function. Broom sorghum can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusing it in hot water to make a medicinal drink.
